title: Permission Grant and Validation Flow
interaction_flow: Client → Router: POST /auth/grant-user-app {app_uid|origin, permission, extra?, meta?}
Router: check actor instanceof UserActorType — throw 'forbidden' if not
Router [if origin]: → AuthService: app_uid_from_origin(origin) → returns app_uid
Router → PermissionService: grant_user_app_permission(actor, app_uid, permission, extra, meta)
PermissionService → _rewrite_permission(permission): iterate _permission_rewriters, apply matching rewrites → returns rewritten permission string
PermissionService → DB: get_app({uid: app_uid}) OR get_app({name: app_uid}) — throw entity_not_found if not found
PermissionService → DB: SELECT 1 FROM user_to_app_permissions WHERE user_id=? AND app_id=? AND permission=? — return early if exists
PermissionService → DB: UPSERT INTO user_to_app_permissions (user_id, app_id, permission, extra)
PermissionService → DB: INSERT INTO audit_user_to_app_permissions (user_id, app_id, permission, action='grant', reason)
#scan → Redis: GET cacheKey — return parsed JSON if hit
#scan [cache miss] → scan-permission.mjs.call(this, {actor, permission_options, reading, state}): iterate PERMISSION_SCANNERS
scan-permission.mjs → PERMISSION_SCANNERS[user-app]: SELECT FROM user_to_app_permissions WHERE user_id=? AND app_id=? AND permission=?
scan-permission.mjs → PERMISSION_SCANNERS[dev-app]: SELECT FROM dev_to_app_permissions WHERE app_id=? AND permission=?
#scan → Redis: SET cacheKey = JSON.stringify(reading) with TTL
#flat_validateUserPerms [hit, not deleted]: return [{$:'option', via:'user', has_terminal:true, permission, ...}]
#linked_validateUserPerms → DB: SELECT * FROM user_to_user_permissions WHERE holder_user_id=? AND permission=?
#linked_validateUserPerms [for each row]: check anti_cycle_actors to skip cycles
#linked_validateUserPerms → PermissionService.scan(issuer_actor, row.permission, state) — recursive issuer check
#linked_validateUserPerms: push {$:'path', via:'user', has_terminal, permission, issuer_username, reading}
validateUserPerms [linked results]: → KVService.set({key: PERM_KEY_PREFIX:user_id:perm, value: {permission, issuer_user_id, data}}) for each option
App → Perms.request(permission): → puter.ui.requestPermission({permission}) — IPC to UI
App → Perms.requestEmail(): whoami() → if no email: ui.requestPermission({permission:'user:{uuid}:email:read'}) → whoami()
key_relationships: Router (grant-user-app.js) depends_on PermissionService via Context services registry
Router depends_on AuthService for origin→app_uid resolution
PermissionService uses PermissionRewriter chain for permission string normalization
PermissionService uses Redis for permission-scan cache (read + write + invalidate)
PermissionService.validateUserPerms uses KVService as fast-path flat cache for user-user perms
PermissionService.validateUserPerms delegates to DB for linked (user_to_user_permissions) lookup
Perms.js (client) wraps all grant/revoke/request calls as HTTP to API routes
Perms.request() delegates to puter.ui.requestPermission (IPC) for end-user consent

data_structures.permission_reading_option: { $:'option', permission, source, by, data, holder_username, issuer_username, has_terminal }
data_structures.permission_reading_path: { $:'path', via, has_terminal, permission, data, holder_username, issuer_username, reading[] }

important_notes: grant_user_app_permission skips write+invalidation if permission already exists (idempotent). _rewrite_permission runs inside Context.sub({is_grant_user_app_permission:true}) as a guard. validateUserPerms uses a two-tier cache: KVService (flat fast-path) then DB (linked with anti-cycle protection). scan() uses Redis with configurable TTL; invalidation is per (user, app, permission) key. PERMISSION_SCANNERS includes 8 named scanners iterated in order by scan-permission.mjs.
